overview.summary: This set contains multiple-choice questions from various chapters, including historiography, mass media, sports, tourism, and heritage management.
youWillLearn: Identify key figures and their contributions to historiography
Recognize important milestones in the history of mass media
Recall facts about sports, tourism, and heritage sites in India
Test knowledge of the subject through MCQ practice

quiz.question: It may be said that ________ was the founder of modern historiography.
quiz.explanation: Voltaire is considered the founder of modern historiography for his critical approach to history.

quiz.question: Balshastri Jambhekar started 'Digdarshan', the first monthly magazine in ________.
quiz.explanation: Balshastri Jambhekar started 'Digdarshan' as the first monthly magazine in Marathi.

quiz.question: Thomas Cook established a travel agency selling ________.
quiz.explanation: Thomas Cook established a travel agency selling tourist tickets.

Who is associated with Marxist Historiography?
Who is associated with Feminist Historiography?

overview.summary: This set requires students to write short notes on topics like Subaltern History, Archives, and Vishwakosh.
youWillLearn: Understand the concept and importance of Subaltern History
Learn about the role and significance of Archives in historical research
Gain knowledge about Encyclopedias (Vishwakosh) as a medium of information
Develop skills for concise note-writing

notes.title: Subaltern History
notes.question: Write a short note on Subaltern History.
notes.qnaAnswer: Subaltern History focuses on the history of the deprivation of all rights. The seeds of subaltern history lie in the Italian importance on the idea that the bottommost rank. Ranjit Guha is an important figure in subaltern history. Similar thoughts were expressed by Mahatma Phule and Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar, which can be found in their writings.

notes.title: Archives
notes.question: Write a short note on Archives.
notes.qnaAnswer: A place where historical documents are preserved is called an 'Archive'. Many old documents, official records, old films, and records of treaties are kept at this place. We get references of original documents because of Archives, making a study of historical events of a particular period possible. We also come to know about the language and script used in a particular period/era. The National Archives of India is in Delhi, the largest Archive in Asia. Every state in India maintains its archives independently.

notes.title: Vishwakosh
notes.question: Write a short note on Vishwakosh.
notes.qnaAnswer: Comprehensive Encyclopaedia (Vishwakosh) means a book which has information on all subjects known, i.e., all subjects under the sun. Vishwakosh is an important medium of documenting and disseminating information. The information is included in encyclopaedia after thorough research and study. There are two types of encyclopaedias - one that includes all subjects like 'Encyclopaedia Britannica', Maharashtra Dnyankosh, Marathi Vishwakosh. The other type includes comprehensive information on one chosen subject, e.g., Bharatiya Samskruti Kosh', 'Vyayan Dnyankosh'.

flashcards.front: What is Subaltern History?
flashcards.back: History from the perspective of the deprived and bottommost ranks of society.

flashcards.front: Who is a key figure associated with Subaltern History?
What is an Archive?
flashcards.back: A place where historical documents, records, and old films are preserved.

flashcards.front: Where is the largest Archive in Asia located?
What is a Vishwakosh?
flashcards.back: A comprehensive encyclopaedia with information on all subjects.

quiz.question: Who is an important figure associated with Subaltern History?
quiz.explanation: Ranjit Guha is a key figure in Subaltern History.

quiz.question: What is the primary function of an Archive?
quiz.explanation: Archives primarily preserve historical documents, records, and films.

quiz.question: Which of the following is an example of a comprehensive encyclopaedia?
quiz.explanation: Encyclopaedia Britannica is a general encyclopaedia covering all subjects.

chapters.title: Set 4: Explain the Statements with Reasons (Any Two)

overview.summary: This set asks students to explain historical statements with reasons, covering topics like travelogues, newspapers, toys, and heritage conservation.
youWillLearn: Understand the historical importance of travelogues like 'Maza Pravas'
Recognize the role of newspapers in education and democracy
Learn how toys can be sources of cultural history
Appreciate the need for trained professionals in heritage preservation

notes.content: Q. 3. Explain the given statements with reasons : (Any Two)

notes.title: Maza Pravas is an important source of history
notes.question: Explain: Maza Pravas is an important source of history.
notes.qnaAnswer: Vishnubhat Godse in his travelogue wrote about his journey to Ayodhya and back to Maharashtra. He published a book, called 'Maza Pravas' in which he gave an eye witness account of the events which unfolded during the Indian War of Independence in 1857. He gave full detailed descriptions about various incidents during that period, especially on the life of the Queen of Jhansi Lakshmibai. His writings give us an idea about the nature of the Marathi language in the 18th century. Therefore, it is considered as an important source of history.

notes.title: Newspaper is an important medium of education and information
notes.question: Explain: Newspaper is an important medium of education and information.
notes.qnaAnswer: Newspapers report events which are interesting to the public. But the importance of newspaper stretches far beyond a passing human interest in events. It covers a miscellany of topical issues. News would involve matters of higher importance like war, global warming, education, national elections or trivial issues such as scandals, gossips and debates on minor controversies. Newspapers have contributed significantly to the spread of literacy and the concept of human rights and democratic freedoms. They are integral to the development of democracy. In fact, they can help in making the democracy stronger. Newspapers not only report the events but continue shape opinions in the global village.

notes.title: Toys can tell us about cultural history
notes.question: Explain: Toys can tell us about cultural history.
notes.qnaAnswer: A tradition of making different type of toys for entertainment is going on since ancient time. Toys give us an idea about the cultural and religious development of that period. Clay models of forts and clay images of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, his soldiers, people and animals are made during Diwali festival in Maharashtra. They are placed on the walls of forts during that period. An ivory doll found at Pompeii, an ancient city in Italy, sheds light on Indo-Roman trade and cultural relations. The mention of games, toys and flying and dancing dolls in Kathasaritsagara give us an idea about cultural history of toys. In this way, we come to know about cultural history from toys.

notes.title: Only trained persons can take up conservation tasks
notes.question: Explain: Only trained persons who are duly qualified can take up the tasks involved in the work of conservation and preservation.
notes.qnaAnswer: While conducting historical research, one has to do the different tasks carefully like collecting sources, preserving, exhibiting, etc. The skill required for every task is different. The procedure followed for each task is different as well as the preliminary preparations. It is essential to know about precautions to be taken. Hence only trained persons who are duly qualified can take up the tasks involved in the work of conservation and preservation.

flashcards.front: Who wrote 'Maza Pravas'?
What historical event does 'Maza Pravas' describe?
flashcards.back: The Indian War of Independence in 1857.

flashcards.front: How do newspapers contribute to democracy?
flashcards.back: By spreading literacy, human rights awareness, and shaping public opinion.

flashcards.front: What can toys reveal about history?
flashcards.back: Cultural and religious development, trade relations, and daily life.

quiz.question: Why is 'Maza Pravas' considered an important historical source?
options: It was written by a British official
It provides an eye-witness account of the 1857 revolt
It describes the life of Shivaji Maharaj
It is the first book published in Marathi
quiz.explanation: 'Maza Pravas' gives an eye-witness account of the events of the 1857 Indian War of Independence.

quiz.question: Which city's ruins yielded an ivory doll indicating Indo-Roman trade?
quiz.explanation: An ivory doll found at Pompeii sheds light on Indo-Roman trade and cultural relations.

quiz.question: Why is specialized training required for conservation and preservation work?
options: Because different tasks require different skills and precautions
Because it is a very old tradition
Because it is only done in India
quiz.explanation: Different tasks like collecting sources, preserving, and exhibiting require different skills, procedures, and precautions, hence only trained persons are qualified.

chapters.title: Set 5: Read the Extract and Answer the Questions

overview.summary: This set is based on a passage about the history of Radio in India, from the Indian Broadcasting Company to All India Radio (Akashvani).
youWillLearn: Trace the history of broadcasting in India from IBC to AIR
Learn about the renaming of AIR to Akashvani
Understand the various types of programs broadcast by Akashvani
Identify the motto and key figures associated with Akashvani

notes.content: Q. 4. Read the following extract and answer the questions given below:

Radio : Indian Broadcasting Company (IBC), a private company was the first one to broadcast daily programmes. Later the same company was taken over by the British Government and named as 'Indian State Broadcasting Service (ISBS). On 8th June 1936 it was renamed, as 'All India Radio (AIR)'. After Independence, AIR became an integral part of the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (India). Initially, it broadcasted Governmental programmes and schemes. It was named as 'Akashvani' on the suggestion of the famous poet Pandit Narendra Sharma. Akashvani broadcasts various entertainment, awareness creating and literary programmes. It also broadcasts special programmes for farmers, workers, the youth and women. The 'Vividh Bharati' programmes are broadcasted in 24 regional languages as well as 146 dialects of Indian languages. Lately, various new channels like 'Radio Mirchi' are providing radio services.

notes.title: Question 1
notes.question: What is the motto of Akashvani?
notes.qnaAnswer: 'Bahujanhiary Bahujansukhay' is the motto of Akashvani.

notes.title: Question 2
notes.question: Who suggested the name 'Akashvani'?
notes.qnaAnswer: The name 'Akashvani' was suggested by the famous poet Pandit Narendra Sharma.

notes.title: Question 3
notes.question: Which programmes are broadcast by Akashvani?
notes.qnaAnswer: Initially, Akashvani broadcasted government programmes and schemes. Later it broadcasted various entertainment and literary programmes. Akashvani presents various programmes for creating awareness. Special programmes are also broadcasted for farmers, workers, youth and women.

flashcards.front: What was the first broadcasting company in India?
When was All India Radio (AIR) established?
Who suggested the name 'Akashvani'?
What is the motto of Akashvani?

quiz.question: What was AIR originally called before being renamed?
quiz.explanation: The Indian Broadcasting Company was taken over by the British Government and named 'Indian State Broadcasting Service (ISBS)' before becoming AIR.

quiz.question: When was All India Radio (AIR) renamed as 'Akashvani'?
quiz.explanation: AIR was renamed to 'Akashvani' on 8th June 1936.

quiz.question: In how many regional languages does Vividh Bharati broadcast?
quiz.explanation: The 'Vividh Bharati' programmes are broadcasted in 24 regional languages.

chapters.title: Set 6: Answer the Questions in Brief (Any Two)

overview.summary: This set contains long-answer questions on feminist historiography, the development of toys, the purpose of tourism, and interpreting historical pictures.
youWillLearn: Understand the concept and impact of feminist historiography
Trace the development of toys and their importance as historical sources
Explore the various purposes and inspirations behind tourism
Learn to observe and interpret historical pictures

notes.content: Q. 5. Answer the following questions in brief : (Any Two)

notes.title: What is feminist historiography?
notes.question: What is feminist historiography?
notes.qnaAnswer: Feminist historiography means writing history with women perspective. There was a lot of dominance of male perspective on historical writing. Simone de Beauvoir, a French scholar, insisted on rethinking on the male dominated perspective and including the women's perspective in historiography. The idea of feminist historiography was accepted. Because of her efforts various aspects of women's lives like their employment, their role in trade unions and their family life became part of historiography.

notes.title: Trace the development of toys and their importance
notes.question: Trace the development of toys and their importance.
notes.qnaAnswer: A tradition of making different type of toys for entertainment is going on since ancient times. Toys can give us an idea about the cultural and religious development of that period. Clay models of forts and clay images of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, his soldiers, people and animals are made during Diwali festival in Maharashtra. They are placed on fort which gives us an idea about the structures of forts during that period. An ivory doll found at Pompeii, an ancient city in Italy, sheds light on Indo-Roman trade and cultural relations. The mention of games, toys and flying and dancing dolls in Kathasaritsagara give us an idea about cultural history of toys. In this way, we come to know about cultural history from toys.

notes.title: Explain the purpose of tourism
notes.question: Explain the purpose of tourism.
notes.qnaAnswer: Tourism has several purposes and inspiration behind it. They are as follows: To see historical monuments forts and cities discovered during excavations. To visit centres promoting ancient art and craft, museums. To visit different pilgrim centres, sea beaches, mountain ranges, valleys, snow-capped peaks, pristine jungles. To visit special plateaus like Kas plateau, museums, lakes formed because of meteoric impact and valleys of flowers, places of natural beauty gives us enjoyment and experiences which connects us to nature. To visit different industrial sites. To see medicinal plants and avail of ayurvedic therapies. To visit places to see agricultural projects, visit to see agricultural research centres and making of compost pits. To travel to watch sports events which comes under sports tourism. To be part of dance-music festival or watch as audience, which is cultural tourism. People also visit places to attend film festivals, witness the making of movies, science seminars, international conferences, book exhibitions and literary events. In short, the purpose of tourism differs from person to person. It is explored as per individual interest.

notes.title: Observe the given picture and write information about it
notes.question: Observe the given picture and write information about it.
notes.qnaAnswer: This picture is of the largest Indian rock-cut Hindu temple located in the Ellora cave. This famous rock-cut Shiva temple was constructed during the reign of Rashtrakuta king Krishna I. It is regarded as a world heritage site by UNESCO in 1983. It bears an eloquent testimony to the high level skill attained by India in the art of sculpture and architecture under the Rashtrakuta patronage. A sculpture of an elephant and a pillar can be seen in the picture. This famous rock cut temple in Maharashtra was declared as a world heritage site by UNESCO in 1983.

flashcards.front: What is the focus of Feminist Historiography?
flashcards.back: Writing history from a women's perspective.

flashcards.front: Who insisted on including women's perspective in historiography?
What do clay models during Diwali in Maharashtra represent?
flashcards.back: Forts and figures related to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj.

flashcards.front: Which text mentions games and flying dolls?
What is the Ellora cave temple dedicated to?

quiz.question: Who is the French scholar associated with feminist historiography?
quiz.explanation: Simone de Beauvoir insisted on rethinking the male-dominated perspective and including women's perspectives in historiography.

quiz.question: What type of tourism involves attending dance and music festivals?
quiz.explanation: Being part of or watching dance-music festivals is a form of cultural tourism.

quiz.question: When was the Ellora cave temple declared a UNESCO World Heritage site?
quiz.explanation: The Ellora cave temple was declared a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1983.

overview.summary: This set covers Political Science topics including the Election Commission, features of movements, the Right to Information, political parties, and the importance of voting.
youWillLearn: Understand the role and structure of the Election Commission in India
Learn about the features and importance of social movements in a democracy
Analyze the impact of the Right to Information Act on governance
Recognize political parties as social organizations
Appreciate voting as a duty and responsibility of citizens

notes.content: Q. 7. State whether the following statements are true or false. Give reasons for your answers: (Any Two)

notes.title: Statement 1
notes.statement: Secrecy in the working of the government had increased due to Right of Information.

notes.title: Reason for Statement 1
notes.question: Explain your answer for Statement 1.
notes.qnaAnswer: The above statement is False. To strengthen democracy and increase mutual trust between the government and the people, it is very important that the people should know about the functioning of the government. Transparency and accountability are the hallmarks of good governance. With Right to Information given to the citizens, Government became more transparent. Thus, the Right to Information has reduced element of secrecy in administration.

notes.title: Reason for Statement 2
notes.question: Explain your answer for Statement 2.
notes.qnaAnswer: The above statement is True. People who have similar views come together voluntarily and form a party, so it can be said that political parties are an integral part of our society. The political parties try to solve problems of the people. Some political parties represent the role of a particular community in society, its ideology and work to achieve it. Hence it can be said that political parties are social organizations.

notes.title: Reason for Statement 3
notes.question: Explain your answer for Statement 3.
notes.qnaAnswer: The above statement is True. People organise themselves and start a movement to social issues. Leaders and activists provide all the information regarding those issues to the government. The government takes notice of the problems because the movement. Some movements aim at opposing some policies the government and certain decisions. This is the recognized method of protest in democracy. Hence, movements have great importance in democracy.

notes.content: Q.8. (A) Explain the following concept : (Any One)

notes.title: Election Commission
notes.question: Explain the concept of the Election Commission.
notes.qnaAnswer: One of the most important features of a democratic nation is elections at regular intervals. Holding free and fair elections at regular intervals is essential for democratic system. Under the Article 324 of the Constitution, Election Commission was formed in 1950. The President appoint one Chief Election Commissioner and two additional commissioners. It is an autonomous body. The rank and powers of all the three commissioners are the same. The declaration of the dates of the elections and the announcement of the results, the entire procedure is monitored by the Election Commission.

notes.title: Features of Movement
notes.question: Explain the features of a movement.
notes.qnaAnswer: A movement is a collective action which has active participation or support of the people. People take up a particular issue and organise themselves. A movement requires strong leadership. A strong leader makes the movement effective. If people feel connected to the issue, then they extend their support to the movement.

notes.title: Voting is our duty as well as responsibility
notes.question: Explain: Voting is our duty as well as responsibility.
notes.qnaAnswer: It is enshrined in the fundamental principles of our Constitution to vote. It is not only our duty but also responsibility. Democracy exists because of elections. People should elect honest and efficient representatives through election. If voters show no interest in voting then the government will ignore people's welfare. Hence I feel it is not only the duty of every citizen to vote but also his responsibility.

notes.title: Women's movement after independence
notes.question: Which issues were taken up by the women's movement after independence?
notes.qnaAnswer: Many issues were taken up by the women's movement for welfare of the women after independence. Some are: Health and education of women, Financial independence, Social security, and To be treated as human beings and have a status and prestige.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Election Commission?
flashcards.back: To conduct free and fair elections in India.

flashcards.front: Under which Article of the Constitution was the Election Commission formed?
What is the hallmark of good governance?
How has the Right to Information affected government secrecy?
flashcards.back: It has reduced the element of secrecy.

flashcards.front: Why are political parties considered social organisations?
flashcards.back: They bring together people with similar views and work for societal problems.

quiz.statement: Secrecy in government increased due to the Right of Information.
quiz.explanation: The Right to Information promotes transparency and reduces secrecy in administration.
Political parties are formed by people with similar views and work for societal problems.

quiz.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
quiz.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner.

quiz.question: What is the recognized method of protest in a democracy?
quiz.explanation: Movements are a recognized method of protest in a democracy.

quiz.question: Who started the first English newspaper in India?

quiz.question: Which is the first newspaper in Marathi?

quiz.question: Where were the ancient Olympic competitions held?

quiz.question: Who was the first Indian female boxer to win an Olympic bronze medal?

quiz.question: Which painting by Leonardo-da-Vinci is in the Louvre Museum?

quiz.question: Where is the first museum in India located?
quiz.explanation: The Indian Museum at Kolkata is the first museum in India.

quiz.question: Which organisation has given directives for preserving cultural and natural heritage?

quiz.explanation: The four pillars of democracy are Legislature, Executive, Judiciary, and Newspaper (Media).

youWillLearn: Recall important names, dates, and facts from history

flashcards.front: Who is considered the founder of modern historiography?
What is the motto of Akashvani?
What is the national game of India?
What are the wooden dolls of Maharashtra called?
What is Bhilar known as?

quiz.sentence: ________ is considered the founder of modern historiography.
quiz.explanation: Voltaire is considered the founder of modern historiography.
'Bahujanhiary Bahujansukhay' is the motto of Akashvani.

quiz.sentence: ________ is the national game of India.
quiz.explanation: Hockey is the national game of India.

quiz.sentence: Bhilar near Mahabaleshwar is known as the 'Village of ________'.
quiz.explanation: Bhilar is known as the 'Village of Books'.

quiz.sentence: The ________ at Kolkata is the first museum in India.
quiz.explanation: The Indian Museum at Kolkata is the first museum in India.

overview.summary: This set focuses on arranging historical events and developments in chronological order.
youWillLearn: Understand the timeline of Indian broadcasting history
Trace the development of mass media in India
Learn the chronological sequence of historical events
Develop chronological thinking skills

notes.content: Arrange the Historical Events in Chronological Order

notes.title: History of Radio in India
items: Indian Broadcasting Company (IBC) starts daily programmes
British Government takes over and names it 'Indian State Broadcasting Service (ISBS)'
Renamed as 'All India Radio (AIR)' on 8th June 1936
AIR becomes part of Ministry of Information and Broadcasting after Independence
Renamed as 'Akashvani' on suggestion of Pandit Narendra Sharma
Vividh Bharati starts broadcasting in 24 regional languages
Radio Mirchi and other private channels start

notes.title: Development of Mass Media in India
items: Start of first English newspaper by James Augustus Hickey
Start of 'Kesari' and 'Maratha' by Agarkar and Tilak
First English news bulletin from Mumbai radio station (1927)

flashcards.front: When was All India Radio (AIR) established?
Which Chinese monk travelled to India in 630 C.E.?
Who wrote 'Mirchchhakatika'?

quiz.question: When was All India Radio (AIR) established?
quiz.explanation: AIR was established on 8th June 1936.

quiz.question: Who wrote the play 'Mirchchhakatika'?
quiz.explanation: Shudraka wrote the play 'Mirchchhakatika'.

quiz.question: When did the first English news bulletin broadcast from Mumbai radio station?
quiz.explanation: The first English news bulletin was broadcast on 23rd July, 1927 from Mumbai.

overview.summary: This set focuses on differentiating between various concepts in History and Political Science.
youWillLearn: Differentiate between types of historiography
Understand the difference between various media forms
Distinguish between types of tourism
Compare different types of heritage

notes.title: Historiography Approaches
rows: History of deprived and bottommost ranks

flashcards.front: What is the focus of Subaltern History?
flashcards.back: History of deprived and bottommost ranks of society.

flashcards.front: What is the focus of Feminist Historiography?
What is the difference between print and electronic media?
flashcards.back: Print media is printed paper (newspapers), electronic media is audio/visual (radio, TV).

quiz.question: What is the focus of Subaltern History?
options: History of deprived and bottommost ranks
quiz.explanation: Subaltern History focuses on the history of deprived and bottommost ranks of society.

quiz.question: Who is associated with Feminist Historiography?
quiz.explanation: Simone de Beauvoir is associated with Feminist Historiography.

quiz.question: Which is an example of Cultural Tourism?
quiz.explanation: Visiting a film festival is an example of Cultural Tourism.

chapters.title: Set 13: Complete the Concept Map and Flow Chart

overview.summary: This set includes concept mapping and flow chart exercises from various chapters in History.
youWillLearn: Organize historical concepts visually
Understand the relationships between different historiographical approaches
Trace the development of mass media in India
Connect sports, tourism, and heritage management concepts

flashcards.front: What are the four pillars of democracy?
Name the types of historiography.
What are the purposes of tourism?
flashcards.back: Historical, pilgrimage, natural, industrial, sports, cultural.

flashcards.front: What are the types of heritage?
flashcards.back: Cultural heritage, Natural heritage, Rock-cut caves, Museums.

quiz.question: Which is NOT one of the four pillars of democracy?
quiz.explanation: The four pillars of democracy are Legislature, Executive, Judiciary, and Newspaper (Media).

quiz.question: Which of the following is a type of historiography?
quiz.explanation: Nationalistic Historiography is one of the types mentioned.

quiz.question: Which of the following is a purpose of tourism?
quiz.explanation: Visiting historical monuments and forts is a purpose of tourism.

quiz.question: Which is an example of cultural heritage?
quiz.explanation: Red Fort is an example of cultural heritage.

overview.summary: This set covers completing series questions from various chapters in History.

quiz.sentence: Complete the series: Voltaire → ________ → Leopold Ranke → Karl Marx
quiz.explanation: Herodotus comes after Voltaire and before Leopold Ranke in the chronological order of historians.

quiz.sentence: Complete the series: James Augustus Hickey → Darpan → ________ → Kesari
quiz.explanation: The chronological order of newspapers is: James Augustus Hickey (first English), Darpan (first Marathi), Prabhakar, then Kesari.

quiz.sentence: Complete the series: IBC → ISBS → ________ → Akashvani
quiz.explanation: The sequence of radio in India: IBC → ISBS → AIR (All India Radio) → Akashvani.

chapters.title: Set 16: Say Right or Wrong (True or False)

overview.summary: This set covers true or false questions from various chapters in History.
youWillLearn: Identify correct and incorrect statements about historical facts
Understand the nuances of historical events and concepts
Test knowledge through true or false exercises

notes.content: Say Right or Wrong (True or False)

notes.title: Statement 1
notes.statement: Voltaire is considered the founder of modern historiography.

notes.title: Statement 2
notes.statement: Balshastri Jambhekar started 'Digdarshan', the first monthly magazine in English.

notes.title: Statement 3
notes.statement: Thomas Cook established a travel agency selling handicrafts.

notes.title: Statement 5
notes.statement: The National Archives of India is in Delhi.

notes.title: Statement 6
notes.statement: The first English newspaper in India was started by James Augustus Hickey.

notes.title: Statement 7
notes.statement: Darpan is the first newspaper in Hindi.

notes.title: Statement 8
notes.statement: The motto of Akashvani is 'Bahujanhiary Bahujansukhay'.

notes.title: Statement 9
notes.statement: Hockey is the national game of India.

notes.title: Statement 10
notes.statement: The wooden dolls made in Maharashtra are known as Thaki.

notes.title: Statement 11
notes.statement: Mary Kom was the first Indian female boxer to win a gold medal in the Olympics.

notes.title: Statement 12
notes.statement: Bhilar is known as the 'Village of Books'.

notes.title: Statement 13
notes.statement: The Indian Museum at Mumbai is the first museum in India.

notes.title: Statement 14
notes.statement: UNESCO has given directives for preserving cultural and natural heritage.

notes.title: Statement 15
notes.statement: Karl Marx is associated with 'Discourse on the Method'.

flashcards.front: Is Voltaire the founder of modern historiography?
Is Chess an outdoor game?
flashcards.back: False, it is an indoor game.

flashcards.front: Is the National Archives of India in Delhi?
Is Darpan the first newspaper in Marathi?
Is Hockey the national game of India?
Is Bhilar known as the 'Village of Books'?

quiz.statement: Voltaire is considered the founder of modern historiography.
quiz.explanation: Voltaire is indeed considered the founder of modern historiography.
Chess is an indoor game, not an outdoor game.

quiz.statement: The National Archives of India is in Delhi.
quiz.explanation: The National Archives of India is indeed located in Delhi.

quiz.statement: Darpan is the first newspaper in Marathi.
quiz.explanation: Darpan is the first newspaper published in Marathi.

quiz.statement: Mary Kom was the first Indian female boxer to win a gold medal in the Olympics.
quiz.explanation: Mary Kom won a bronze medal, not a gold medal.

quiz.statement: The Indian Museum at Mumbai is the first museum in India.
quiz.explanation: The Indian Museum at Kolkata is the first museum in India.

quiz.statement: UNESCO has given directives for preserving cultural and natural heritage.
quiz.explanation: UNESCO has given directives for the preservation of cultural and natural heritage.
